data.command: export PATH="$HOME/go/bin:$HOME/bin:$PATH" && gt prime --hook && gt mail check --inject

message.content:
[GAS TOWN] deacon <- daemon • 2026-02-23T12:11 • patrol

I am Deacon. Start patrol: run gt deacon heartbeat, then check gt hook. If no hook, create mol-deacon-patrol wisp and execute it.

content.thinking:
There are some tmux sessions that look like they might be orphaned or leftover:
- `0`, `111`, `203` - numbered sessions, possibly orphaned
- `aq-refinery`, `aq-witness` - rig sessions for an "aq" rig
- `cl-refinery`, `cl-witness` - rig sessions for a "cl" rig
- `hq-boot` - boot session

But the `gt status` said "No rigs registered." So aq-witness, aq-refinery, cl-witness, cl-refinery might be orphaned sessions from unregistered rigs. 

The numbered sessions are also worth flagging, though I should be careful here—these could legitimately belong to the user. I'll document what I'm seeing but avoid any destructive actions until I understand the situation better.

content.text: Found several tmux sessions outside of deacon/mayor: numbered sessions (0, 111, 203), rig sessions (aq-refinery, aq-witness, cl-refinery, cl-witness), and hq-boot. No rigs are registered per `gt status`, so the rig sessions may be orphaned. Not taking destructive action — noting for awareness.
